title: MSc in Bioinformatics
description: The MSc in Bioinformatics is a comprehensive two-year program that trains students in computational biology, genomics, and data analysis. Students gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge bioinformatics tools and techniques, preparing them for careers in research, healthcare, and biotechnology.
overview: A rigorous graduate program combining molecular biology, computer science, and statistics to analyze biological data.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in biological sciences, computer science, mathematics, or related field
Minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale
requirements: English proficiency test scores (TOEFL/IELTS for international students)
learningOutcomes: Analyze and interpret large-scale biological datasets
Apply statistical methods to biological problems
Design and conduct computational biology research

title: PhD in Data-Intensive Sciences
description: The PhD in Data-Intensive Sciences is an advanced research program that prepares students to tackle complex problems in bioinformatics, data science, and computational methods. Students conduct original research under the guidance of experienced faculty and contribute to advancing the field.
overview: A doctoral program focusing on cutting-edge research in data science, bioinformatics, and high-performance computing.
eligibility: Master's degree in bioinformatics, computer science, statistics, or related field
Strong academic record with minimum GPA of 3.5
Research experience demonstrated through publications or thesis work
Proficiency in programming and statistical analysis
requirements: Official transcripts from all previous institutions
Three letters of recommendation from academic referees
learningOutcomes: Develop novel computational methods and tools
Contribute to advancing knowledge in the field

title: Introduction to Bioinformatics Workshop
description: A hands-on five-day workshop introducing participants to the fundamentals of bioinformatics. Learn essential skills in sequence analysis, genomic data processing, and using popular bioinformatics tools.
overview: A practical introduction to bioinformatics for researchers and students.
eligibility: Background in biological sciences or related field
learningOutcomes: Understand basic bioinformatics concepts and workflows
Use BLAST and sequence alignment tools
Process and analyze DNA/RNA sequence data

title: Data Science Bootcamp
description: An intensive two-week bootcamp covering essential data science skills including programming in Python/R, data visualization, statistical analysis, and machine learning fundamentals.
overview: Comprehensive training in data science tools and techniques for researchers and professionals.
eligibility: University degree or equivalent professional experience
learningOutcomes: Write Python/R programs for data analysis
Apply statistical methods to real datasets

title: HPC Training Course
description: Learn how to use high-performance computing resources for research. This course covers cluster architecture, job scheduling, parallel programming, and optimizing computational workflows.
overview: Practical training in using HPC systems for scientific computing.
eligibility: Graduate students or researchers with computational projects
Programming experience in at least one language
requirements: Active research project requiring HPC resources
learningOutcomes: Use common scientific software on HPC systems

title: Research Internship Program
description: A 3-6 month research internship program for undergraduate students interested in computational biology and data science. Interns work alongside faculty and graduate students on active research projects.
overview: Hands-on research experience for undergraduate students in bioinformatics and data science.
requirements: Letter of recommendation from a faculty member
learningOutcomes: Learn bioinformatics and data analysis skills
Work in a collaborative research environment
